description_long: This is an irregularly shaped, roughly rectangular stone with a single, cleanly bored circular hole near its upper center. The stone's surface is uneven and pitted, with visible natural texturing and subtle color variations ranging from light tan to muted brown. The edges are rounded and slightly chipped, suggesting some degree of weathering or handling. The hole appears intentionally made, possibly for mounting, tethering, or decorative purposes. The stone is relatively thin compared to its height and width, as seen from the side views. Its overall appearance suggests it could serve as a functional or decorative urban barrier or marker.
description: Irregular tan-brown stone with a central circular hole and rough, weathered surface. Thin profile and rounded edges suggest use as a barrier or marker.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full face of the stone with a prominent circular hole near the top and visible surface texture.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the stone's thin profile and uneven thickness, with rough, natural edges.
description_view_2: Back view similar to the front, displaying the hole and surface texture, but with slightly different edge contours.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again highlighting the stone's thinness and irregular edge profile.
attribute_placement: set as a standalone feature or barrier, often upright or partially embedded
attribute_pattern: Irregular natural mottling and pitting, with no artificial carvings or regular patterns; the hole is the only geometric feature.
attribute_weathering: Surface is rough and slightly chipped, indicating moderate weathering and exposure to the elements.
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Typically contrasts with paved surfaces but may blend with other natural stones or landscaping elements; can be partially surrounded by grass or soil.
